reasoning:
The question asks whether a vaccine will have been developed for Middle East respiratory syndrome (MERS) according to the English Wikipedia page 'List of infectious diseases' on 2026-04-05. The forecast date is 2026-03-29, 7 days before resolution. 
The background indicated the status as of 2026-03-19 was 'No'.
I checked the section of the Wikipedia page. The list is very long and got truncated before reaching MERS, but a web search for recent news surrounding a MERS vaccine found results from January/February 2026 stating 'The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), the European Medicines Agency (EMA), the World Health Organization (WHO), and the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia have not approved a Middle East Respiratory Syndrome Coronavirus (MERS-CoV) vaccine candidate as of January 2026' and that the WHO also published a global summary on January 29, 2026 indicating that there is no vaccine available. Another article from CEPI in March 2025 indicated that vaccines were just entering preclinical trials. A recent study (January 9, 2026) was just evaluating a phase 1b clinical trial. Thus, no vaccine has been developed. Because the resolution date is extremely close (7 days) and clinical trials take years, it is effectively impossible for a genuine vaccine to be approved and added to Wikipedia before 2026-04-05. I submit the lowest allowed probability.
